def: Covered in fog, so that it is hard to see far.
A person who belongs to your family.
Feeling pleased and satisfied about something you or others have done.
Feeling sudden wonder because something unexpected has happened.
A formal written request, for example to an office or authority.
Money in the form of coins and notes, not card or digital.
The food eaten at one time, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
A thick liquid used for cooking or in engines and machines.
A short coat worn over other clothes.
A long piece of cloth worn around the neck for warmth.
Liked by many people.
A very short period of time.
What is true and matches the facts.
A good result that you achieve after effort.
The way in which two things are not the same.
The money paid regularly to live in a home you do not own.
An object used by hand to do work, such as a hammer or drill.
A shop where you buy medicines and health products.
In or near the middle; easy to reach from many places.
A public show of art or objects for people to look at.
A local form of a language, with its own words and pronunciation.
A small folding case for carrying money and cards.
A pain felt in the head.
A term of study spent at a university in another country.
A baked bread snack twisted into a knot and sprinkled with salt.
A ticket that lets you enter an event or place.
A school where young people learn a trade alongside an apprenticeship.
A letter sent with a job application to introduce yourself.
A hotel deal that includes breakfast and one other meal.
A marked path for walking in nature.
The low land between hills or mountains.
To rest and get your health or energy back.
An office that gives visitors advice and maps about an area.
To draw or mark something onto a map or plan.
The arrangement of things one after another.
Objects added to make a place look attractive.
The evening of 24 December, before Christmas Day.
A stick of wax with a wick that gives light when burned.
A celebration marking the start of the new year.
A custom or belief passed down over many years.
To make something look nicer by adding objects or ornaments.
The successful finishing of a course of university study.
A place where you are able to park a car.
A route by bus, train, or road linking two places.
A meal where dishes are set out for people to serve themselves.
Goose cooked in the oven, a traditional festive dish.
To fill a room or home with furniture.
One level of a building.
A large container you fill with water to sit and wash in.
The direction where the sun rises.
A lively public festival with costumes, music, and parades.
A large, important church.
The experience gained from living or working in another country.
A group of similar things gathered together.
A look at how two or more things are alike or different.
To be in a particular place.
To feel comfortable and content.
A Christian festival in spring celebrating the resurrection of Jesus.
The Christian festival on 25 December celebrating the birth of Jesus.
A piece of business carried out with a bank, such as a transfer.
The desk in a bank where staff serve customers.
The account information needed to send or receive money.
A bank card that pays directly from your account.
A bank account for keeping money and earning interest.
The extra money a bank pays on savings or charges on a loan.
A small individual part or fact of something.
What is contained inside something.
To start speaking to someone.
The body's natural defence against illness and infection.
A fibre that carries signals between the brain and the body.
The part of the body where the arm joins the trunk.
A card showing that you are covered by health insurance.
A controlled way of eating, often to lose weight or stay healthy.
A medical treatment that protects you against a disease.
A room equipped for scientific experiments and tests.
A person who studies or works in science.
A test done to discover or prove something.
The feeling of needing rest or sleep.
To make the loud rumbling sound that follows lightning.
For water to fall from the clouds in drops.
With strong winds and rough weather.
The part of a piece of clothing that covers the arm.
The part of a shirt or coat that fits around the neck.
A jacket made of leather.
An open shoe with straps, worn in warm weather.
A warm, heavy coat for cold weather.
Having a pattern of stripes.
A large round container for storing and serving beer.
An open-air market selling second-hand goods.
The flavour of food, or a person's sense of style.
A piece of jewellery worn on the ear.
A spare bed for visitors to sleep in.
Somewhere that a person can sleep.
A famous building or object that a place is known for.
The number of people who visit a place.
A film that shows real people and facts, not a made-up story.
A person who makes films.
The real place where an event actually happened.
A celebration held for the first showing of a film or play.
A person who is on a journey.
A message that tells you about a possible danger.
Keen on travelling and eager to see new places.
Known by people all over the world.
An offer to study a subject at a university.
A full-time school that trains students for a specific profession.
A set of questions or tasks used to check knowledge or quality.
A person who tests eyes and sells glasses.
A person trained to care for and teach young children.
A person whose job is to advise on the law and represent people in court.
A person who designs and builds machines, structures, or systems.
A skill or certificate showing that you are able to do something.
To keep learning new skills after finishing school or training.
A woman trained to care for sick or injured people.
How good and comfortable a person's life is.
The act of deciding in advance how to do something.
A set of ideas meant to explain how or why something happens.
The facts and understanding that a person has.
To pay attention to a sound or to what someone says.
Feeling worried and under pressure.
The papers you send when applying for a job.
Your name written in your own way to sign something.
A system that lets employees choose their working hours within limits.
The making of goods in factories on a large scale.
A place at work or school where meals are served.
To sell and send goods to another country.
A course that improves your skills for your job.
The things a person knows about a subject.
Extra training taken to improve skills or knowledge.
A first explanation that presents a subject or a person.
The money spent on travelling somewhere.
